reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by submitting a block with nil or invalid deposit request, causing the validation error to return as raw execReqErr (L516-517) instead of being wrapped in invalidBlock. This bypasses proper block invalidation in fork choice, potentially allowing invalid blocks to propagate through the network. All gates passed: code is active (called from receive_block.go:222), entry point is untrusted P2P network, and finding is in-scope for Fusaka audit (consensus block validation). Severity maintained at High due to consensus-critical nature of deposit validation bypass in Prysm (31% consensus client share).
